title.zh: 擁抱越多，改變越少？破解社會網絡中的「影響力悖論」
title.en: The Influence Paradox: Why Being Well-Connected Doesn’t Mean You’re Effective

summary.zh: 這項發現對理解領導力、創新採用及公共政策設計具有深遠意義。它提醒決策者，單純追求「影響力最大」的節點並非萬靈丹，網絡的整體結構同樣關鍵。如果忽略網絡的聚集特性，即使選對了「關鍵人物」，也可能因為結構性的阻礙而讓努力付諸東流。未來的社會干預需要從單純的「點狀打擊」轉向對整個網絡生態的更細緻調控。
summary.en: These findings carry significant implications for understanding leadership, innovation adoption, and public policy design. They serve as a reminder that simply chasing the most 'influential' nodes is not a panacea; network structure matters just as much. Ignoring clustering characteristics can render efforts futile, even when the right individuals are targeted. Future social interventions must shift from isolated 'point strikes' to a more nuanced regulation of the entire network ecosystem.

keyArticle.title: The Influence “Paradox”: When More Network Ties Lead to Less Change
